title: Neuroplasticity
definition: The brain's ability to reorganize itself by forming new neural connections throughout life.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The brain has the power to change and adapt, learning from experiences and healing from injuries.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Neural connections in the brain can grow or diminish based on use and experience, demonstrating that our brains are malleable throughout life.
difficulty_levels.expert: The adult brain is not fixed but dynamic, constantly rewiring itself in response to experience. This ongoing process challenges traditional views of personal identity as a fixed essence, suggesting instead that it is a continuous and evolving process.

extra_fields.insight: Our brains are not static but fluid, constantly changing and adapting throughout our lives.
